mission.title: contour: Redirect: adding a redirect (301 or 302) option in ingressroute / httpproxy
mission.description: Redirect: adding a redirect (301 or 302) option in ingressroute / httpproxy. Requested by 11+ users.

steps.title: Check current contour deployment
steps.description:
Verify your contour version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n projectcontour -l app.kubernetes.io/name=contour
helm list -n projectcontour 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working contour installation.

steps.title: Review contour configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant contour configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n projectcontour -l app.kubernetes.io/name=contour
kubectl get configmap -n projectcontour -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=contour
```
The ingressroute / httpproxy should be able to support a match and then redirect with either a 301 or 302 to an http or https destination

Something like:
```
spec:
  routes:
  - match: /
    services:
    - name: awesome-service-01
      port:

steps.title: Apply the fix for Redirect: adding a redirect (301 or 302) option in…
steps.description:
add a HTTPRequestRedirectPolicy to the HTTPProxy spec which allows for HTTPProxy.Routes to specify a requestRedirectPolicy. This policy will allow a redirect to be configured for a specific set of Conditions within a single route.
```yaml
spec:
  routes:
  - match: /
    services:
    - name: awesome-service-01
      port: 1000
   - match: /redirectpath
    redirect:
      method: 301
      destination: https://externalawesomewebsite.com
  virtualhost:
    fqdn: awesomehost.tld
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n projectcontour -l app.kubernetes.io/name=contour
kubectl get events -n projectcontour --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Redirect: adding a redirect (301 or 302) option in…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: add a HTTPRequestRedirectPolicy to the HTTPProxy spec which allows for HTTPProxy.Routes to specify a requestRedirectPolicy. This policy will allow a redirect to be configured for a specific set of Conditions within a single route.
codeSnippets:
spec:
  routes:
  - match: /
    services:
    - name: awesome-service-01
      port: 1000
   - match: /redirectpath
    redirect:
      method: 301
      destination: https://externalawesomewebsite.com
  virtualhost:
    fqdn: awesomehost.tld

spec:
  virtualhost: foo.example.com
  routes:
    - redirect:
        destination: bar.example.com  # required
        include_query_string: false      # default

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with contour installed or the issue environment reproducible.
